zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull於1465年出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾也斯特時，她的父親Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld35歲，母親Lady Marion Stewart8歲。她於1498年與John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1524年在蘇格蘭培爾金努爾享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull于1465年出生在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾也斯特时，她的父亲Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld35岁，母亲Lady Marion Stewart8岁。她于1498年与John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1524年在苏格兰培爾金努爾享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: Crichton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Creighton
meanings: Scottish and Irish: habitational name from Crichton, near Edinburgh, first recorded c. 1128 in the form Crectune, in 1287 as Crecton, and in 1360 as Creychtona. The name is probably an early hybrid compound of Old Welsh creic ‘rock’ + Older Scots tun ‘farm, settlement’ (Old English tūn). In Ireland and Britain, this form of the name is now found chiefly in northern Ireland; the more usual Scottish forms are Crichton and Crighton .
Irish: sometimes used for Gaelic Ó Creacháin or Ó Criocháin (see Crehan ).
English: habitational name from Creighton in Staffordshire, named with Celtic creig ‘rock’ + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull was born in 1465, in Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, her father, Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld, was 35 and her mother, Lady Marion Stewart, was 8. She married John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee in 1498, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. She died in 1524, in Kinnoull, Perthshire, Scotland, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull est née en 1465, à Yester, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, son père, Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Lady Marion Stewart, avait 8 ans. Elle a épousé John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee en 1498, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée en 1524, à Kinnoull, Perthshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull 1465 in Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld 35 und ihre Mutter Lady Marion Stewart 8. Sie hat 1498 in Scotland John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb 1524 im Alter von 59 Jahren in Kinnoull, Perthshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoullが1465年にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアイエスターで生まれた時，父Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilldは35歳，母Lady Marion Stewartは8歳であった。1498年にスコットランドでJohn Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundeeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1524年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアキノウルで59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull(이)가 1465년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 예스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Marion Stewart(은)는 8세였다. 그녀는 1498년 스컷랜드에서 John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1524년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 킨우에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull nasceu em 1465, em Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, seu pai, Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Lady Marion Stewart, tinha 8 anos. Ela casou-se com John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee em 1498, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1524, em Kinnoull, Perthshire, Scotland, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull родилась в 1465 г. в местности иестир, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Marion Stewart, 8. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee в 1498 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла в 1524 г. в местности кинноулл, пиртшир, Шотландия в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull nació en 1465, en Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, su papá, Robert Crichton- I. Fourth Lord Crichton Of Sanquhar Of Kennwilld, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Lady Marion Stewart, tenía 8 años. Se casó con John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee en 1498, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió en 1524, en Kinnoull, Perthshire, Scotland, a la edad de 59 años.
